title: Java Cheatsheet - Complete Reference
description: Master Java from basics to advanced with a unified Swiggy-style food delivery example throughout

concepts.name: Your First Java Program
concepts.explanation: Every Java program starts with a class and a main method. Think of the class as a Restaurant building, and main() as the front entrance where customers (Java) enter to start their food ordering journey.
keyPoints: public = anyone can access (like a public restaurant)
static = belongs to the class itself, not specific objects
main = Java's entry point - execution starts here
String[] args = command-line arguments for the program
Every Java file must have a class with same name as filename
Interview: Why is main static? So JVM can call it without creating object

concepts.name: Primitive Data Types
concepts.explanation: Java's building blocks for storing simple values. In our Swiggy app, we use different types to store order quantities, prices, ratings, and status flags.
keyPoints: int is default for whole numbers (4 bytes, -2B to 2B)
double is default for decimals (8 bytes, high precision)
boolean for yes/no flags (true/false only, not 0/1)
char uses single quotes: 'A', String uses double: "Hello"
byte/short rarely used - int is preferred for integers
Default values: int=0, double=0.0, boolean=false, char='\u0000'
Interview: What's difference between float and double? Precision (32-bit vs 64-bit)

concepts.name: Variables and Constants
concepts.explanation: Variables store data that can change (like order status), while constants store fixed values (like GST rate, platform fee).
keyPoints: Variables declared with: dataType variableName = value;
Constants use 'final' keyword - cannot be reassigned
Constant names in UPPER_SNAKE_CASE by convention
Use constants for values that never change (rates, limits)
final prevents reassignment but doesn't make objects immutable
Local variables must be initialized before use
Interview: Can you change final variable? No, compilation error

concepts.name: Type Casting
concepts.explanation: Converting one data type to another. Like converting total bill (double) to rounded rupees (int), or expanding small quantity (byte) to larger count (int).
keyPoints: Implicit casting: smaller → larger type (automatic, safe)
Explicit casting: larger → smaller type (manual, may lose data)
Narrowing: reverse direction requires explicit cast
Casting int to double before division prevents integer division
Be careful: casting large long to int may overflow
Interview: What's difference between implicit and explicit casting?

concepts.name: Arithmetic Operators
concepts.explanation: Basic math operations for calculating order totals, discounts, delivery fees, and splitting bills.
keyPoints: / division (int/int gives int, use double for decimals)
Use parentheses to control order: (a + b) * c
Interview: Difference between i++ and ++i? Post vs pre-increment
i++ returns old value then increments, ++i increments then returns

concepts.name: Comparison Operators
concepts.explanation: Compare values to make decisions - check if order qualifies for free delivery, if restaurant is highly rated, if customer is eligible for discounts.
keyPoints: >= greater than or equal, <= less than or equal
Interview: Why not use == for Strings? Compares references, not content

concepts.name: Logical Operators
concepts.explanation: Combine multiple conditions - check if customer is premium AND order is above threshold, or if restaurant is open OR has late-night delivery.
keyPoints: && (AND): true only if BOTH conditions are true
|| (OR): true if AT LEAST ONE condition is true
! (NOT): inverts boolean value (true → false, false → true)
Short-circuit: && stops if first is false, || stops if first is true
Use parentheses for clarity: (a && b) || c
Interview: What is short-circuit evaluation? Stops evaluating once result is determined

concepts.name: Ternary Operator
concepts.explanation: Compact if-else in one line - quickly assign delivery fee based on order value, or set order priority based on customer type.
keyPoints: Returns a value (can assign to variable)
More concise than if-else for simple assignments
Both value expressions must be same type
Interview: When to use ternary? Simple conditional assignments only

concepts.name: if-else Statements
concepts.explanation: Make decisions based on conditions - apply discounts, check delivery eligibility, validate orders.
keyPoints: if executes block only if condition is true
else executes when if condition is false
else-if for multiple conditions (checked in order)
Use curly braces {} even for single statements (best practice)
First true condition executes, rest are skipped
Interview: What happens if no condition is true? Nothing executes (unless else exists)

concepts.name: switch Statement
concepts.explanation: Choose between multiple options based on a value - handle order status, payment methods, delivery zones.
keyPoints: switch compares value against multiple cases
break prevents fall-through to next case
default executes if no case matches (optional but recommended)
Works with: byte, short, int, char, String, enum
Cannot use: long, float, double, boolean
Interview: What happens without break? Fall-through to next case
Java 12+: switch expressions with -> (no break needed)

concepts.name: for Loop
concepts.explanation: Repeat code a specific number of times - process menu items, calculate totals, display order history.
keyPoints: Use when you know number of iterations
i++ is most common update (increment by 1)
Can use i-- to count backwards
Interview: What's scope of loop variable? Inside loop only (if declared in for)

concepts.name: Enhanced for Loop (for-each)
concepts.explanation: Iterate through collections easily - process all menu items, customers, orders without index management.
keyPoints: Cannot access index (use regular for if needed)
Works with arrays and collections (ArrayList, etc.)
Interview: When to use for-each? When you need to process all elements sequentially

concepts.name: while Loop
concepts.explanation: Repeat while condition is true - wait for order confirmation, process queue, retry failed operations.
keyPoints: May not execute at all if condition is initially false
Use when number of iterations is unknown
Be careful: infinite loop if condition never becomes false
Update condition inside loop to avoid infinite loop
Interview: Difference between while and do-while? while checks condition first

concepts.name: do-while Loop
concepts.explanation: Execute at least once, then repeat while condition is true - show menu at least once, validate input.
keyPoints: Executes body at least once before checking condition
Use when you need to execute at least once
Common use: input validation, menu systems
Interview: When to use do-while? When body must execute at least once

concepts.name: break and continue
concepts.explanation: Control loop flow - exit early when order found, skip cancelled orders, stop on error.
keyPoints: continue: skips current iteration, moves to next
break works with for, while, do-while, switch
continue works with for, while, do-while only
In nested loops, break/continue affects innermost loop only
Use labeled break/continue for outer loops (rare)
Interview: Difference between break and continue? break exits, continue skips

concepts.name: Array Basics
concepts.explanation: Fixed-size containers for storing multiple values of same type - menu items, prices, customer ratings.
keyPoints: Fixed size - cannot grow or shrink after creation
Initialize with values: Type[] name = {val1, val2, ...};
Default values: int=0, double=0.0, boolean=false, objects=null
Interview: Array vs ArrayList? Array fixed size, ArrayList dynamic

concepts.name: Multi-dimensional Arrays
concepts.explanation: Arrays of arrays - restaurant menu with categories, delivery zones with areas, order history by month.
keyPoints: Jagged arrays allowed (rows of different lengths)
Interview: How to find length? array.length for rows, array[i].length for columns

concepts.name: Array Utility Methods
concepts.explanation: Java provides Arrays class with utility methods for sorting, searching, comparing, and filling arrays.
keyPoints: Arrays.sort() sorts in ascending order (modifies original)
Arrays.binarySearch() requires sorted array, returns index or negative
Arrays.fill() sets all elements to specified value
Arrays.copyOf() creates new array with copied elements
Arrays.toString() converts array to readable string
Time complexity: sort O(n log n), binarySearch O(log n)
Interview: How to sort in descending order? Use Collections.reverseOrder() with wrapper types

concepts.name: String Basics
concepts.explanation: Strings are immutable sequences of characters - customer names, addresses, menu items, order IDs.
concepts.code:
String name = "Rahul Kumar";

int len = name.length();  // 11
char first = name.charAt(0);  // 'R'

String firstName = name.substring(0, 5);  // "Rahul"
String upper = name.toUpperCase();  // "RAHUL KUMAR"

String orderId = "ORD" + "12345";  // Concatenation
keyPoints: Strings are immutable - cannot be changed after creation
String pool: literals stored in special memory area
charAt() throws StringIndexOutOfBoundsException if invalid index
Interview: Why are Strings immutable? Security, thread-safety, caching

concepts.name: String Methods
concepts.explanation: Common string operations for validation, formatting, searching in customer data and menu items.
keyPoints: trim() removes leading and trailing whitespace
isEmpty() checks if length is 0, isBlank() checks if only whitespace
Interview: Difference between isEmpty() and isBlank()? isBlank checks whitespace too

concepts.name: StringBuilder
concepts.explanation: Mutable string for efficient concatenation - building order summaries, generating receipts, creating dynamic messages.
keyPoints: StringBuilder is mutable - can be modified
More efficient than String concatenation in loops
Interview: String vs StringBuilder? String immutable, StringBuilder mutable and efficient for concatenation

concepts.name: Classes and Objects
concepts.explanation: Classes are blueprints, objects are instances. A Restaurant class defines what all restaurants have (name, rating, menu), and each restaurant object (McDonald's, KFC) has its own specific values.
keyPoints: Constructor initializes object (same name as class, no return type)
this keyword refers to current object
new keyword creates object and calls constructor
Each object has its own copy of instance variables
Interview: What is 'this'? Reference to current object

concepts.name: Encapsulation
concepts.explanation: Hide internal details and provide controlled access - protect customer balance, validate order quantities, ensure data integrity.
keyPoints: private = accessible only within the class
Setters provide write access with validation: setFieldName()
Allows changing internal implementation without affecting users
Interview: Why encapsulation? Data hiding, validation, flexibility

concepts.name: Inheritance
concepts.explanation: Child class inherits properties and methods from parent class - DeliveryPartner and RestaurantOwner both inherit from User, avoiding code duplication.
keyPoints: Child inherits all non-private members from parent
super() calls parent constructor (must be first line)
protected = accessible in same package and subclasses
Java supports single inheritance only (one parent)
Interview: Why single inheritance? Avoids diamond problem

concepts.name: Polymorphism - Method Overriding
concepts.explanation: Child class provides specific implementation of parent method - different order types calculate total differently.
keyPoints: Method overriding: child provides specific implementation
Method signature must match parent (name, parameters, return type)
Runtime polymorphism: method called based on actual object type
Parent reference can hold child object: Order order = new PremiumOrder()
Overridden method called based on object, not reference type
Cannot override: final, static, private methods
Interview: Overriding vs Overloading? Overriding = same signature in child, Overloading = same name different parameters

concepts.name: Polymorphism - Method Overloading
concepts.explanation: Multiple methods with same name but different parameters - addItem() can add single item, multiple items, or item with quantity.
keyPoints: Method overloading: same name, different parameters
Parameters must differ in: number, type, or order
Return type alone doesn't distinguish methods
Compiler chooses method based on arguments
Interview: Can you overload main()? Yes, but JVM only calls main(String[] args)

concepts.name: Abstraction - Abstract Classes
concepts.explanation: Abstract classes provide partial implementation - Payment is abstract, specific payment methods (UPI, Card, Cash) implement the details.
keyPoints: Can have abstract methods (no body) and concrete methods (with body)
Child must implement all abstract methods or be abstract itself
Can have constructors, fields, and concrete methods
Use when classes share common behavior but differ in implementation
Can extend only one abstract class
Interview: Abstract class vs Interface? Abstract can have state and concrete methods

concepts.name: Abstraction - Interfaces
concepts.explanation: Interfaces define contracts - what a class must do, not how. Trackable interface ensures Order and DeliveryPartner can both be tracked.
keyPoints: All methods public and abstract by default
All fields public, static, and final by default
Java 8+: can have default and static methods
Interview: When to use interface? When unrelated classes need same behavior

concepts.name: Access Modifiers
concepts.explanation: Control visibility of classes, methods, and fields - protect sensitive data, expose public API.
keyPoints: private: accessible only within the class
protected: accessible in same package and subclasses
default (no modifier): accessible in same package only
Use private for fields, public for methods (generally)
Interview: Access modifier order? public > protected > default > private

concepts.name: try-catch-finally
concepts.explanation: Handle errors gracefully without crashing - handle payment failures, invalid orders, network issues.
keyPoints: try: code that might throw exception
Multiple catch blocks: specific to general
finally executes even if return in try/catch
Use e.printStackTrace() for full stack trace
Interview: When does finally not execute? System.exit() or JVM crash

concepts.name: throw and throws
concepts.explanation: Manually throw exceptions or declare that method might throw - validate order data, enforce business rules.
keyPoints: throws: declares method might throw exception
Checked exceptions must be handled or declared
Can throw multiple exception types: throws IOException, SQLException
Interview: Checked vs Unchecked? Checked must be handled, Unchecked are runtime errors

concepts.name: Custom Exceptions
concepts.explanation: Create domain-specific exceptions - InvalidOrderException, PaymentFailedException, RestaurantClosedException.
keyPoints: Can add custom fields and methods
Makes code more readable and maintainable
Interview: When to create custom exception? When built-in exceptions don't convey business meaning

concepts.name: ArrayList
concepts.explanation: Dynamic array that grows/shrinks - store menu items, orders, customers. Most commonly used collection.
keyPoints: Initial capacity: 10, grows by 50% when full
Interview: ArrayList vs Array? ArrayList dynamic, Array fixed size

concepts.name: LinkedList
concepts.explanation: Doubly-linked list - efficient insertion/deletion at ends. Good for queue of pending orders, delivery partner assignments.
keyPoints: More memory per element (stores prev/next references)
Can be used as Stack, Queue, or Deque
Interview: ArrayList vs LinkedList? ArrayList for random access, LinkedList for frequent insertions/deletions

concepts.name: HashMap
concepts.explanation: Key-value pairs for fast lookup - restaurant ID to Restaurant object, customer ID to Customer, order ID to Order.
keyPoints: Keys must be unique (duplicate key overwrites value)
Fast operations: O(1) average for get/put/remove
Grows when size > capacity * load factor
Allows one null key and multiple null values
Interview: How HashMap works? Uses hashing, stores in buckets, handles collisions with linked list/tree

concepts.name: HashSet
concepts.explanation: Unique elements only - store unique customer IDs, restaurant tags, order statuses. No duplicates allowed.
keyPoints: Fast operations: O(1) average for add/remove/contains
Interview: HashSet vs TreeSet? HashSet faster but unordered, TreeSet sorted but slower

concepts.name: TreeSet
concepts.explanation: Sorted set - automatically sorts elements. Store restaurants by rating, orders by time, menu items alphabetically.
keyPoints: Sorted set - maintains elements in sorted order
Elements must be Comparable or provide Comparator
Use when sorted order is required
Interview: TreeSet vs HashSet? TreeSet sorted O(log n), HashSet unsorted O(1)

concepts.name: LinkedHashMap
concepts.explanation: HashMap with insertion order - track order of menu items added, maintain customer visit history.
keyPoints: Slightly slower than HashMap due to linked list overhead
Allows one null key and multiple null values
Interview: HashMap vs LinkedHashMap? LinkedHashMap maintains order, HashMap doesn't

concepts.name: TreeMap
concepts.explanation: Sorted map - automatically sorts by keys. Store restaurants by rating, menu items by price, orders by timestamp.
keyPoints: Sorted map - maintains keys in sorted order
Does not allow null keys (throws NullPointerException)
Keys must be Comparable or provide Comparator
Use when sorted key order is required
Interview: TreeMap vs HashMap? TreeMap sorted O(log n), HashMap unsorted O(1)

concepts.name: PriorityQueue
concepts.explanation: Queue with priority - process high-priority orders first, assign urgent deliveries, handle VIP customers.
keyPoints: Priority queue - elements ordered by priority
Operations: O(log n) for add/poll, O(1) for peek
Elements must be Comparable or provide Comparator
Interview: When to use PriorityQueue? When you need to process elements by priority, not insertion order

concepts.name: Stack
concepts.explanation: LIFO (Last In First Out) - undo operations, navigation history, expression evaluation.
keyPoints: push() adds to top, pop() removes from top
empty() checks if stack is empty
Interview: Stack vs Queue? Stack LIFO, Queue FIFO

concepts.name: Queue and Deque
concepts.explanation: FIFO queue and double-ended queue - order processing queue, delivery partner assignment, task scheduling.
keyPoints: offer() adds, poll() removes, peek() views
Deque: can add/remove from both ends
ArrayDeque more efficient than LinkedList for queue/stack
Interview: When to use Deque? When you need to add/remove from both ends

concepts.name: Generic Classes
concepts.explanation: Type-safe classes that work with any type - create reusable Order container, Response wrapper, Cache implementation.
keyPoints: Generics provide type safety at compile time
Type parameter naming: T (Type), E (Element), K (Key), V (Value)
Can have multiple type parameters: <K, V>
Type erasure: generic type info removed at runtime
Cannot create generic array: new T[10] is invalid
Interview: Why generics? Type safety, code reusability, eliminates casting

concepts.name: Generic Methods
concepts.explanation: Methods that work with different types - utility methods for printing, comparing, converting data.
keyPoints: Generic methods can be in non-generic classes
Type parameter declared before return type: <T> void method()
Type inference: compiler determines type from arguments
Interview: Generic method vs Generic class? Method has type parameter in method signature

concepts.name: Wildcards
concepts.explanation: Flexible generic types - accept any type of list, work with unknown types, create flexible APIs.
keyPoints: ? extends T = upper bounded (T or subclasses)
? super T = lower bounded (T or superclasses)
Use extends for reading, super for writing
Cannot add elements to ? extends (except null)
Interview: When to use wildcards? When you don't care about specific type

concepts.name: Lambda Expressions
concepts.explanation: Concise way to write anonymous functions - filter orders, sort restaurants, process menu items functionally.
keyPoints: Curly braces required for multiple statements
return keyword required for multiple statements
Can access final or effectively final variables
Interview: What is lambda? Anonymous function that implements functional interface

concepts.name: Functional Interfaces
concepts.explanation: Interfaces with single abstract method - Predicate for filtering, Function for transformation, Consumer for processing.
keyPoints: Functional interface: interface with single abstract method (SAM)
Can have default and static methods
Interview: Common functional interfaces? Predicate, Function, Consumer, Supplier

concepts.name: Method References
concepts.explanation: Shorthand for lambda expressions - reference existing methods instead of writing lambda.
keyPoints: More readable than lambda for simple cases
Order::new same as id -> new Order(id)
Interview: When to use method reference? When lambda just calls existing method

concepts.name: Stream Basics
concepts.explanation: Functional-style operations on collections - filter orders, calculate totals, transform data without loops.
keyPoints: Stream: sequence of elements supporting sequential and parallel operations
Streams are lazy - operations executed only when terminal operation called
Intermediate operations: filter, map, sorted (return Stream)
Terminal operations: collect, forEach, count (return result)
Streams can be used only once
Don't modify source during stream operations
Interview: Stream vs Collection? Stream for processing, Collection for storage

concepts.name: Stream Operations
concepts.explanation: Common stream operations - sorting, limiting, skipping, distinct, reducing data.
keyPoints: sorted() sorts in natural order, sorted(comparator) for custom
reduce() combines elements into single result
findFirst() returns first match, findAny() for parallel streams
Interview: What is reduce? Combines stream elements using associative function

concepts.name: Collectors
concepts.explanation: Collect stream results into different forms - lists, sets, maps, grouping, partitioning.
keyPoints: Collectors.toList(), toSet(), toMap() for basic collections
groupingBy() groups elements by classifier function
summarizingDouble() provides all statistics at once
Interview: groupingBy vs partitioningBy? groupingBy for multiple groups, partitioningBy for two groups

concepts.name: Creating Threads
concepts.explanation: Run multiple tasks concurrently - process multiple orders simultaneously, handle multiple delivery partners, parallel restaurant updates.
keyPoints: Two ways: extend Thread or implement Runnable
start() creates new thread and calls run()
Calling run() directly doesn't create new thread
Interview: Thread vs Runnable? Runnable more flexible, allows multiple inheritance

concepts.name: Thread Synchronization
concepts.explanation: Prevent race conditions when multiple threads access shared data - protect order counter, wallet balance, inventory.
keyPoints: synchronized prevents multiple threads from executing code simultaneously
Only one thread can execute synchronized code at a time
join() waits for thread to complete
Prevents race conditions but may cause performance overhead
Interview: What is race condition? Multiple threads accessing shared data causing inconsistent results

concepts.name: Executor Framework
concepts.explanation: Manage thread pools efficiently - handle multiple orders without creating too many threads, reuse threads.
keyPoints: awaitTermination() waits for tasks to complete
Interview: Why use Executor? Better thread management, reusability, control

concepts.name: Concurrent Collections
concepts.explanation: Thread-safe collections - share data between threads safely without explicit synchronization.
keyPoints: ConcurrentHashMap: thread-safe HashMap, better than synchronized HashMap
CopyOnWriteArrayList: thread-safe ArrayList, good for read-heavy scenarios
BlockingQueue: thread-safe queue with blocking operations
Interview: ConcurrentHashMap vs Hashtable? ConcurrentHashMap allows concurrent reads, Hashtable locks entire map

concepts.name: Reading and Writing Files
concepts.explanation: Read/write order data, save customer information, export reports, load configuration.
keyPoints: Use try-with-resources for automatic resource closing
Files.readAllLines() reads entire file into List
Interview: Why try-with-resources? Automatically closes resources, prevents leaks

concepts.name: Serialization
concepts.explanation: Convert objects to bytes for storage/transmission - save Order objects, cache Customer data, send data over network.
keyPoints: Implement Serializable interface to make class serializable
transient keyword excludes field from serialization
Interview: What is serialization? Converting object to byte stream for storage/transmission

concepts.name: static Keyword
concepts.explanation: Belongs to class, not instance - shared counter for all orders, utility methods, constants.
keyPoints: static variable: shared by all instances
static method: called on class, not object
static block: initialization code, runs once
Cannot access instance members from static context
Can access static members from instance context
Interview: When to use static? Utility methods, constants, shared data

concepts.name: final Keyword
concepts.explanation: Prevents modification - constant values, prevent method overriding, prevent inheritance.
keyPoints: final variable: constant, cannot be reassigned
final method: cannot be overridden by subclasses
final parameter: cannot be reassigned in method
final with objects: reference cannot change, but object can be modified
Interview: Why make class final? Prevent inheritance, ensure immutability

concepts.name: this and super Keywords
concepts.explanation: Reference current and parent objects - distinguish parameters from fields, call parent constructors/methods.
keyPoints: this(): call another constructor in same class
super(): call parent constructor (must be first line)
Interview: When to use this? Distinguish parameter from field, return current object

concepts.name: Built-in Annotations
concepts.explanation: Metadata for code - provide information to compiler, runtime, or tools. Common in frameworks like Spring, Hibernate.
keyPoints: @Deprecated: marks code as outdated, warns users
@FunctionalInterface: ensures interface has exactly one abstract method
Interview: Why @Override? Catches typos, ensures correct overriding

concepts.name: Custom Annotations
concepts.explanation: Create your own annotations - mark methods for logging, validation, security checks, caching.
keyPoints: @Retention: how long annotation is retained (SOURCE, CLASS, RUNTIME)
@Target: where annotation can be applied (TYPE, METHOD, FIELD, etc.)
RetentionPolicy.RUNTIME: available at runtime via reflection
Can have elements (methods) with default values
Interview: Retention policies? SOURCE (compile-time), CLASS (bytecode), RUNTIME (reflection)

concepts.name: Meta-Annotations
concepts.explanation: Annotations for annotations - control how custom annotations behave.
keyPoints: @Target: TYPE, FIELD, METHOD, PARAMETER, CONSTRUCTOR, etc.
@Repeatable: allows multiple annotations of same type

concepts.name: Framework Annotations (Spring Example)
concepts.explanation: Real-world annotation usage - Spring Framework uses annotations extensively for dependency injection, REST APIs, transactions.
keyPoints: Interview: Why annotations in Spring? Reduce XML config, cleaner code, convention over configuration

concepts.name: LocalDate, LocalTime, LocalDateTime
concepts.explanation: Modern date/time API - track order timestamps, delivery schedules, restaurant opening hours.
keyPoints: Interview: Why use new API over Date? Immutable, thread-safe, clearer API

concepts.name: Duration and Period
concepts.explanation: Measure time differences - calculate delivery time, order age, subscription duration.
keyPoints: to*() methods convert to specific units
Use Duration for time, Period for dates
Interview: Duration vs Period? Duration for time, Period for dates

concepts.name: Wrapper Classes & Autoboxing
concepts.explanation: Object wrappers for primitives - store order counts in collections, handle null values, use utility methods.
keyPoints: Wrapper classes: Integer, Double, Boolean, Character, etc.
parseInt(), parseDouble() convert String to primitive
Interview: Why wrappers? Collections need objects, not primitives

concepts.name: Optional for Null Safety
concepts.explanation: Avoid NullPointerException - handle missing customer data, optional delivery instructions, nullable restaurant info.
keyPoints: Optional: container that may or may not contain value
of(): creates Optional with non-null value
filter(): keep value if matches condition
Interview: Why Optional? Explicit handling of absence, avoid NullPointerException

concepts.name: Math Class
concepts.explanation: Mathematical operations - calculate distances, round prices, generate order IDs, compute ratings.
concepts.code:
int max = Math.max(100, 200);  // 200
int min = Math.min(100, 200);  // 100
int abs = Math.abs(-50);       // 50

double pow = Math.pow(5, 2);   // 25.0
double sqrt = Math.sqrt(25);   // 5.0

double price = 299.567;
long rounded = Math.round(price);  // 300
double ceil = Math.ceil(price);    // 300.0
double floor = Math.floor(price);  // 299.0
keyPoints: Interview: How to generate random int in range? (int)(Math.random() * range) + min

concepts.name: Random Class
concepts.explanation: Better random number generation - generate order IDs, select random restaurants, create test data.
keyPoints: nextInt(bound): random int from 0 to bound-1
Can set seed for reproducible sequences
Interview: Random vs Math.random()? Random class more flexible, can set seed

concepts.name: var Keyword (Java 10+)
concepts.explanation: Type inference for local variables - cleaner code, less repetition, compiler infers type.
keyPoints: Compiler infers type from right-hand side
Only for local variables (not fields, parameters, returns)
Type is still static (not dynamic like JavaScript)
Interview: Is var dynamic typing? No, still static - type inferred at compile time

concepts.name: Text Blocks (Java 15+)
concepts.explanation: Multi-line strings - JSON responses, SQL queries, HTML templates, formatted receipts.
keyPoints: No need to escape quotes or add \n
Great for JSON, SQL, HTML, formatted text
Interview: Why text blocks? Cleaner multi-line strings, better readability

concepts.name: Switch Expressions (Java 14+)
concepts.explanation: Enhanced switch with return values - cleaner code, no break needed, pattern matching.
keyPoints: Must be exhaustive (cover all cases or have default)
Interview: Switch expression vs statement? Expression returns value, no break needed

concepts.name: Records (Java 16+)
concepts.explanation: Immutable data carriers - DTOs for orders, customer info, menu items. Auto-generates constructor, getters, equals, hashCode, toString.
keyPoints: Auto-generates: constructor, getters, equals, hashCode, toString
Syntax: record Name(Type field1, Type field2, ...) {}
Getters have same name as fields (no 'get' prefix)
Cannot extend other classes (but can implement interfaces)
Interview: When to use records? Immutable data carriers, DTOs, value objects

concepts.name: String Methods (Java 11)
concepts.explanation: New String methods - isBlank, lines, strip, repeat for text processing.
keyPoints: isBlank(): true if empty or only whitespace (Java 11)
stripLeading(), stripTrailing(): remove from one side
strip vs trim: strip handles Unicode whitespace
Interview: isBlank vs isEmpty? isBlank checks whitespace too

concepts.name: HTTP Client (Java 11)
concepts.explanation: Modern HTTP client - async requests to restaurant APIs, payment gateways.
keyPoints: Interview: Why HttpClient? Modern, async, HTTP/2 support

concepts.name: Lambda Var Syntax (Java 11)
concepts.explanation: Use var in lambda parameters - add annotations to lambda params.
keyPoints: Must use var for all params or none
Interview: Why var in lambdas? To add annotations

concepts.name: instanceof Pattern Matching (Java 16)
concepts.explanation: Pattern matching with instanceof - no explicit cast needed.
keyPoints: Combines type check and cast in one step
Pattern variable scoped to if block
Interview: Benefits? Less code, no explicit cast, type-safe

concepts.name: NullPointerException Messages (Java 14)
concepts.explanation: Helpful NPE messages - shows exactly which variable was null.
keyPoints: Shows exactly which variable/field was null
Interview: How does it help? Pinpoints exact null reference

concepts.name: Module System Enhancements (Java 11-17)
concepts.explanation: Java Platform Module System (JPMS) improvements - better encapsulation, explicit dependencies.
keyPoints: Module system: explicit dependencies and encapsulation
Interview: Benefits? Explicit dependencies, better security, smaller runtime

concepts.name: Vector API (Java 16+ Incubator)
concepts.explanation: SIMD operations - parallel data processing for performance-critical calculations.
keyPoints: Vector API: SIMD operations (incubator since Java 16)
Process multiple data elements in parallel
Use for: image processing, ML, scientific computing
Interview: When to use? Performance-critical numerical operations

concepts.name: Stream Enhancements (Java 16-23)
concepts.explanation: New Stream methods - toList(), mapMulti(), takeWhile(), dropWhile().
keyPoints: takeWhile(): take elements while condition true (Java 9)
dropWhile(): drop elements while condition true (Java 9)
Interview: toList() vs collect(toList())? Shorter, returns immutable list

concepts.name: Sealed Classes (Java 17+)
concepts.explanation: Restrict inheritance - define allowed subclasses for order status, payment methods, user types.
keyPoints: Sealed classes: restrict which classes can extend (Java 17+)
Syntax: sealed class Name permits Class1, Class2, ...
Permitted subclasses must be: final, sealed, or non-sealed
All permitted classes must be in same module/package
Enables exhaustive pattern matching in switch
Use for: closed hierarchies, state machines, type-safe enums
Interview: Why sealed classes? Control inheritance, exhaustive pattern matching

concepts.name: Enhanced Pseudo-Random Generators (Java 17)
concepts.explanation: New random number generator API - better algorithms, thread-safe, splittable for parallel streams.
keyPoints: Interview: vs Random? Better algorithms, splittable, more options

concepts.name: Deprecation of Finalization (Java 18)
concepts.explanation: Finalize() deprecated - use try-with-resources, Cleaner API for resource cleanup.
keyPoints: Use Cleaner API for non-deterministic cleanup
Cleaner is safer and more predictable
Interview: Why deprecated? Unreliable, performance issues, better alternatives exist

concepts.name: Simple Web Server (Java 18)
concepts.explanation: Built-in HTTP server for testing - serve static files, prototyping, development.
keyPoints: Interview: Use case? Quick testing, serving static files during development

concepts.name: UTF-8 by Default (Java 18)
concepts.explanation: UTF-8 is now default charset - consistent behavior across platforms.
keyPoints: Interview: Why important? Consistent cross-platform behavior

concepts.name: Code Snippets in Javadoc (Java 18)
concepts.explanation: New @snippet tag for better code examples in documentation.
keyPoints: Interview: vs @code? Better formatting, external files, validation

concepts.name: Virtual Threads (Java 21)
concepts.explanation: Lightweight threads - handle millions of concurrent orders, API calls efficiently.
keyPoints: Can create millions without memory issues
Perfect for I/O-bound tasks (API calls, DB queries)
Blocking operations don't block OS threads
Interview: Virtual vs Platform threads? Virtual are lightweight, millions possible

concepts.name: Sequenced Collections (Java 21)
concepts.explanation: Common interface for ordered collections - first, last, reversed operations.
keyPoints: Implemented by: List, Deque, LinkedHashSet, LinkedHashMap
Interview: Why needed? Unified API for ordered collections

concepts.name: Pattern Matching for switch (Java 21)
concepts.explanation: Pattern matching in switch - type patterns, guards, null handling.
keyPoints: Interview: Benefits? Type-safe, less code, guards for conditions

concepts.name: String Templates (Java 21 Preview)
concepts.explanation: String interpolation - embed variables directly in strings safely.
keyPoints: Interview: vs concatenation? Cleaner, safer, type-checked

concepts.name: Structured Concurrency (Java 21 Preview)
concepts.explanation: Manage concurrent tasks as a unit - fetch menu, check inventory, calculate price together.
keyPoints: Treats concurrent tasks as single unit
ShutdownOnFailure: cancels all if one fails
ShutdownOnSuccess: cancels all when one succeeds
Interview: Benefits? Simpler concurrency, automatic cleanup, better errors

concepts.name: Scoped Values (Java 21 Preview)
concepts.explanation: Share immutable data across threads - user context, request ID, trace ID.
keyPoints: Scoped values: immutable data sharing (Java 21 preview)
Better for virtual threads than ThreadLocal
Use for: user context, request ID, trace ID
Interview: vs ThreadLocal? Immutable, auto cleanup, better for virtual threads

concepts.name: Record Patterns (Java 21)
concepts.explanation: Destructure records in pattern matching - extract order details directly.
keyPoints: Can nest patterns for nested records
Interview: Benefits? Less boilerplate, direct access to components

concepts.name: Implicitly Declared Classes (Java 21 Preview)
concepts.explanation: Simplified main methods for beginners - no class declaration needed.
keyPoints: Interview: Purpose? Lower barrier for beginners, scripting

concepts.name: Key Encapsulation Mechanism API (Java 21)
concepts.explanation: KEM API for quantum-resistant cryptography - secure key exchange.
keyPoints: Interview: Why important? Quantum computing threat, future-proof security

concepts.name: Generational ZGC (Java 21)
concepts.explanation: Improved Z Garbage Collector - generational collection for better performance.
keyPoints: Interview: vs G1GC? Lower latency, better for large heaps

concepts.name: Unnamed Variables & Patterns (Java 22)
concepts.explanation: Use _ for unused variables - cleaner code when you don't need all values.
keyPoints: Shows intent - this value is not used
Works in: lambdas, patterns, catch, for loops
Can have multiple _ in same scope
Interview: Why _? Shows unused intent, cleaner code

concepts.name: Foreign Function & Memory API (Java 22)
concepts.explanation: Call native code safely - integrate with C libraries, system APIs.
keyPoints: Call native C/C++ code from Java
Direct memory access without JNI overhead
Interview: vs JNI? Safer, faster, no native compilation

concepts.name: Stream Gatherers (Java 22 Preview)
concepts.explanation: Custom stream operations - sliding windows, batching orders, custom aggregations.
keyPoints: Stream gatherers: custom stream operations (Java 22 preview)
windowSliding(n): sliding window of size n
Interview: vs Collectors? More flexible, intermediate operation

concepts.name: Class-File API (Java 22 Preview)
concepts.explanation: Parse, generate, transform class files - bytecode manipulation without ASM.
keyPoints: Replaces ASM, ByteBuddy for simple cases
Interview: Use case? Bytecode manipulation, instrumentation, code generation

concepts.name: Statements before super() (Java 22 Preview)
concepts.explanation: Execute statements before calling super() in constructors - validate parameters first.
keyPoints: Can call static methods, validate params
Interview: Why useful? Parameter validation before parent initialization

concepts.name: Flexible Constructor Bodies (Java 22 Preview)
concepts.explanation: More flexible constructor initialization - prepare data before super() or this().
keyPoints: Cannot access instance members before this()/super()
Interview: Benefit? Data preparation before delegation

concepts.name: Primitive Types in Patterns (Java 23 Preview)
concepts.explanation: Pattern matching for primitive types - type-safe numeric conversions.
keyPoints: Interview: Why needed? Type-safe primitive handling in patterns

concepts.name: Markdown in Javadoc (Java 23 Preview)
concepts.explanation: Write Javadoc comments in Markdown format - easier documentation.
keyPoints: Supports: headers, lists, code blocks, bold, italic
Interview: Benefit? Easier to write, more readable in source

concepts.name: Deprecate Unsafe Memory Access (Java 23+)
concepts.explanation: sun.misc.Unsafe being restricted - use Foreign Function & Memory API instead.
keyPoints: Use Foreign Function & Memory API instead
Interview: Why deprecate Unsafe? Safety, maintainability, better alternatives

concepts.name: Synchronizing Virtual Threads (Java 24)
concepts.explanation: Virtual threads no longer pin to platform threads when using synchronized - improved scalability.
keyPoints: Virtual threads no longer pin with synchronized (Java 24)
Now: virtual thread releases platform thread
Can use synchronized freely with virtual threads
No need to refactor to ReentrantLock
Interview: What was pinning? Virtual thread blocking platform thread, limiting concurrency

concepts.name: Ahead-of-Time Class Loading (Java 24)
concepts.explanation: Cache loaded classes for faster startup - monitor first run, reuse in subsequent runs.
keyPoints: AOT class loading: cache loaded classes (Java 24)
Interview: Use case? Microservices, serverless, fast startup needed

concepts.name: Key Derivation Function API (Java 25)
concepts.explanation: Standard API for password-based key derivation - PBKDF2, Argon2, HKDF.
keyPoints: KDF API: derive keys from passwords (Java 25)
Use for: password hashing, key derivation
Interview: Why KDF? Slow down brute-force attacks, secure password storage

concepts.name: Quantum-Resistant Cryptography (Java 24-25)
concepts.explanation: ML-KEM and ML-DSA algorithms - protect against future quantum computing attacks.
keyPoints: Use for: secure communications, data integrity
Interview: Why needed? Quantum computers will break RSA/ECC

concepts.name: Stable Value API (Java 25 Preview)
concepts.explanation: Lazy initialization with thread-safe caching - compute once, use many times.
keyPoints: Interview: vs lazy initialization? Thread-safe, simpler, no double-checked locking

concepts.name: Module Import Declarations (Java 25 Preview)
concepts.explanation: Import entire modules instead of individual classes - cleaner imports.
keyPoints: Module imports: import entire module (Java 25 preview)
Can cause ambiguity - use specific imports to resolve
Interview: Benefit? Cleaner code, explicit module dependencies

concepts.name: Compact Object Headers (Java 24 Experimental)
concepts.explanation: Reduced object header size - 64 bits instead of 96-128 bits, saves memory.
keyPoints: Interview: How? Encodes class pointer and mark word together

concepts.name: Generational Shenandoah GC (Java 25)
concepts.explanation: Shenandoah with generational collection - better throughput and pause times.
keyPoints: Interview: vs non-generational? Better performance, most objects die young

concepts.name: JFR Enhancements (Java 24-25)
concepts.explanation: Java Flight Recorder improvements - CPU-time profiling, method timing, cooperative sampling.
keyPoints: JFR CPU-time profiling: track CPU usage per method (Java 25)
Method timing: record all method calls (Java 25)
Interview: Why JFR? Low-overhead production profiling

concepts.name: PEM Cryptographic Objects (Java 25 Preview)
concepts.explanation: Read/write PEM-encoded keys and certificates - no third-party libraries needed.
keyPoints: PEM support: read/write PEM format (Java 25 preview)
Interview: What is PEM? Privacy-Enhanced Mail format, Base64-encoded

concepts.name: Stream Gatherers Finalized (Java 24)
concepts.explanation: Custom stream operations now stable - windowing, batching, custom aggregations.
keyPoints: Interview: Use case? Windowing, batching, custom stream operations

concepts.name: Class-File API Finalized (Java 24)
concepts.explanation: Standard API for parsing and generating class files - replaces ASM library.
keyPoints: Replaces ASM library for simple cases
Use for: bytecode manipulation, code generation
Interview: vs ASM? Standard API, type-safe, maintained by JDK
